mnemonics: H-A-D-O-O-P: Huge Amounts of Data, Open-source Operations Platform
Use Case: Think 'Logs, Lots of Logs'
keyPoints: Hadoop is designed for distributed storage and processing of very large datasets across clusters of computers.
A common use case is log processing, where web servers generate massive, semi-structured log files that need to be analyzed.
Its core components, HDFS (for storage) and MapReduce (for processing), allow it to scale horizontally and handle petabytes of data.
commonMistakes: Assuming Hadoop is for small, real-time data (it's for large-scale, batch processing).
Confusing Hadoop with a traditional database (it uses a write-once, read-many model, not SQL transactions).
